name: Shillong
description:
Shillong is the capital city of Meghalaya in northeastern India and is widely known as the “Scotland of the East” because of its rolling hills, cool climate, waterfalls, pine forests, and scenic landscapes. The city is famous for its natural beauty, music culture, colonial-era charm, and peaceful atmosphere. Shillong serves as a major gateway for exploring Meghalaya’s mountains, caves, rivers, and living root bridges. The city is also known for its vibrant cafés, local Khasi culture, and frequent music festivals, making it highly popular among nature lovers, backpackers, photographers, and road-trip travelers. Shillong offers a refreshing combination of adventure, relaxation, and cultural exploration, especially for travelers seeking experiences beyond mainstream Indian tourist cities.

---
best_time_why: The weather remains cool, pleasant, and suitable for sightseeing, waterfalls, trekking, café visits, and road trips during these months. Monsoon season brings heavy rainfall that can affect outdoor activities and transportation.
budget_breakdown:
A realistic traveler in Shillong can expect daily expenses around ₹7,000–₹8,000 ($85–$95), depending on accommodation style and nearby excursions. Transportation forms a moderate part of the budget because many tourist attractions are located outside the main city and require taxis or road travel. Accommodation costs remain reasonable compared to larger Indian metropolitan cities, although boutique stays and scenic resorts can increase prices. Food expenses are manageable because Shillong has both affordable local eateries and modern cafés serving international cuisine. Adventure activities and nearby day trips to Cherrapunji or Dawki can slightly increase overall daily spending. Travelers can reduce expenses by using shared cabs and booking accommodations in advance during tourist seasons.

---

cost_breakdown.description: Includes domestic flights to Guwahati, shared taxis, buses, or road transportation to Shillong. International travelers should additionally consider passport, visa, and international airfare expenses.
Covers average guesthouses, boutique hotels, or comfortable mid-range stays within Shillong city. Premium hill resorts may increase overall accommodation costs.
Includes local Khasi meals, café food, snacks, tea, and moderate dining experiences around the city.
Covers sightseeing costs, waterfalls, boating, trekking areas, museums, and nearby nature attractions.
Includes local taxis, shared cabs, and short-distance transportation for sightseeing around Shillong and nearby locations.

food_items.name: Jadoh
food_items.description: A traditional Khasi rice dish prepared using rice, meat, herbs, and local spices. It is considered one of Shillong’s most famous and authentic local meals.

food_items.name: Dohneiiong
food_items.description: A Khasi pork curry cooked with black sesame seeds, giving the dish its distinctive rich flavor and dark texture. It is highly popular among local communities.

food_items.name: Tungrymbai
food_items.description: A fermented soybean dish prepared with local spices, onions, and pork. It represents traditional Khasi tribal cuisine and unique northeastern flavors.

food_items.name: Momos and Thukpa
food_items.description: Due to northeastern and Tibetan culinary influences, Shillong is famous for momos, noodle soups, and warm street-food dishes ideal for cold weather.

food_items.name: Local Café Desserts and Coffee
food_items.description: Shillong’s café culture is highly popular among young travelers and includes pastries, coffee, pancakes, and baked snacks served in cozy hill cafés.

food_areas.name: Police Bazaar
food_areas.specialty: Street food, cafés, and local Khasi cuisine

food_areas.name: Laitumkhrah
food_areas.specialty: Modern cafés, desserts, and youth café culture

food_areas.name: Bara Bazaar
food_areas.specialty: Traditional local food and fresh regional produce

buy_items.name: Traditional Khasi Handicrafts
buy_items.description: Handmade bamboo products, woven baskets, and traditional tribal crafts reflect Meghalaya’s indigenous cultural heritage and craftsmanship.

buy_items.name: Handwoven Shawls and Textiles
buy_items.description: Colorful northeastern shawls and locally woven fabrics are popular souvenirs because of their unique tribal designs and warmth.

buy_items.name: Organic Spices and Honey
buy_items.description: Meghalaya is known for organic agricultural products including spices, local honey, and herbal products produced in hill regions.

buy_items.name: Musical Instruments and Music Merchandise
buy_items.description: Shillong’s strong music culture makes guitars, local music merchandise, and handcrafted instruments popular purchases among tourists.

buy_items.name: Bamboo and Cane Products
buy_items.description: Decorative household items, lamps, baskets, and furniture made from bamboo and cane are widely available in local markets.

buy_items.name: Traditional Jewelry and Accessories
buy_items.description: Handmade tribal jewelry, beadwork, and traditional accessories are commonly purchased by visitors exploring local Khasi markets.

shopping_markets.name: Bara Bazaar
shopping_markets.specialty: Traditional Khasi products and local markets

shopping_markets.name: Lewduh Market
shopping_markets.specialty: Bamboo crafts, textiles, and local produce

travel_advisories: Shillong experiences sudden weather changes and frequent rainfall even outside monsoon season, so carrying rain protection and layered clothing is highly recommended.
Many tourist attractions around Shillong involve hilly roads, trekking paths, and outdoor exploration, therefore comfortable walking shoes are essential.
Road travel in Meghalaya can sometimes be slow because of mountain routes and weather conditions, so travelers should keep flexible schedules for day trips.
Mobile network connectivity can become weak in remote hill areas outside Shillong city, especially during long road journeys and trekking routes.

locations.name: Umiam Lake
locations.description: Umiam Lake is one of Shillong’s most scenic attractions located just before entering the city. Surrounded by pine-covered hills and calm waters, the lake is famous for boating, photography, and peaceful landscapes.

locations.name: Elephant Falls
locations.description: Elephant Falls is one of Shillong’s most famous waterfalls known for its multi-level cascading water and lush green surroundings. It is among the most visited natural attractions in Meghalaya.

locations.name: Shillong Peak
locations.description: Shillong Peak is the highest viewpoint in Meghalaya and offers panoramic views of Shillong city, surrounding hills, and distant Himalayan landscapes during clear weather conditions.

locations.name: Ward’s Lake
locations.description: Ward’s Lake is a beautiful man-made lake located in central Shillong and surrounded by gardens, walking paths, and flowering landscapes. It is popular for boating and family-friendly relaxation.

locations.name: Don Bosco Museum
locations.description: Don Bosco Museum is one of Northeast India’s most important cultural museums showcasing the traditions, history, tribal life, and heritage of northeastern states through modern exhibitions and galleries.
